summary.intent: Implement task #218: a framework-agnostic DropZone core component for the `ars-components` Rust library, including state machine, MIME validation, callbacks, ConnectApi attributes, snapshots, spec conformance, proptest, coverage, and mutation testing.
summary.outcome: The DropZone core was fully implemented with all verification gates passing (18 unit tests, spec conformance, proptest, 96.34% line coverage, 0 missed mutants out of 124). The commit was made but the PR push was the final step in the transcript.

repo: The spec file is authoritative over issue body text when they conflict — issue text had stale `aria-dropeffect` and `data-ars-dragging-over` while the spec correctly uses `aria-description` and `data-ars-drag-over`.
All time/delay fields must use `core::time::Duration`, never `u32` millisecond aliases (e.g., `activate_delay: Duration` not `activate_delay_ms: u32`).

The announcement pattern follows Tabs: the agnostic `Api` exposes localized announcement string accessors (`drop_accepted_announcement()`, `drop_rejected_announcement()`) for adapters to pass to their live region — discarding them with `let _announcement = ...` is incorrect.
GitHub Project board item must be moved to `In Progress` as the first mutating action when starting implementation.
Adapter specs (Leptos and Dioxus) must be updated in the same PR as the core spec when public API changes.
The repo has mandatory `.agents/skills/post-implementation-audit` and `.agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review` workflows that must be invoked.
`cargo xci --profile fast` is the required pre-push gate; a clean pass is required before committing.
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` is the correct snapshot validation command for `ars-components`.
`cargo xtask spec validate` validates all spec markdown files (340 files).
The `ars-interactions::{DragItem, DropOperation}` types are reused directly in component public APIs without re-wrapping.

code.finding: Effects originally discarded announcement strings with `let _announcement = ...`. The correct pattern (matching Tabs) is to expose them via `Api::drop_accepted_announcement()` and `Api::drop_rejected_announcement()` so adapters can pass them to a live region.
Public delay props use `core::time::Duration` with `Duration::from_millis(500)` and `Duration::from_millis(1500)` defaults, not `u32` millisecond fields.

workflow: TDD red-green loop: write failing tests first targeting the public API, then implement the machine until green — this was enforced as a prerequisite before implementation.

Mutation testing with `cargo mutants` is a mandatory audit step; 31 missed mutants on the first pass required adding focused builder, prop-sync, boundary, and API dispatch tests before the second pass achieved 0 missed.

After `cargo xci --profile fast`, check `git status` before committing — the formatter can stage unrelated files (e.g., `crates/ars-dioxus/tests/attr_passthrough.rs`) that must be restored to keep the PR scope clean.

Transient test failures in the fast gate (e.g., `ars-e2e` fixture server timeout) should be reproduced in isolation before rerunning the full gate — a clean isolated rerun indicating a flake justifies rerunning the gate.
Mutation run duration scales with test suite timeout: 124 mutants × ~82s timeout = ~61 minutes for the full run; plan for this in workflow.
Co-authored-by trailers and non-draft PR are explicit repo expectations for commits from automated sessions.

friction: Mutation testing took ~61 minutes for 124 mutants, blocking the implementation loop for an extended period.
First mutation pass had 31 missed mutants (builder surface, prop syncing, boundary checks) requiring a second round of test writing and another full mutation run.
`cargo xci --profile fast` staged an unrelated formatter change in `crates/ars-dioxus/tests/attr_passthrough.rs`, requiring manual `git restore` before commit.
Transient failure in `ars-e2e::fixtures::tests::fixture_server_wait_reports_timeout_output_and_removes_log` during fast gate required a full re-run of `cargo xci --profile fast`.

Issue body had multiple stale API references (`aria-dropeffect`, `data-ars-dragging-over`, missing `on_drop_rejected`) that diverged from the current spec, requiring explicit contract resolution before planning.
Plan Mode restrictions prevented any file mutations during the planning phase, extending the session length before implementation could begin.
The `let _announcement = ...` antipattern was not caught until user code review, meaning announcement API design was deferred to a review finding rather than being planned upfront.
open_items: Leptos and Dioxus adapter implementations for DropZone are explicitly out of scope and deferred to future tasks.
Browser event extraction, DOM listeners, file handle access, hit testing, and live element references remain adapter/platform concerns not implemented here.
Codex review loop (`waiting-for-codex-review`) was not yet started — transcript ends at commit, before PR push and `@codex review` invocation.
PR push to GitHub and opening the non-draft PR with `Closes #218` and `snapshot-reviewed` label was the final deferred step.
Issue #218 remains open until the PR is merged with green CI and Codex thumbs-up.
